source_document.document_identity_notes: Regulator-source PDF from Wisconsin DFI. Cover page confirms MosquitoNix Franchise, LLC 2025 FDD. Issuance date April 30, 2025.

brand.parent_company_notes: FW-MQX Holdings, LLC (Michigan) and O'Neal Family Trust (Texas). FW-MQX Holdings' parent is Franworth, LLC (Michigan). Predecessor: FEMO Group, LP (Texas) operated mosquito businesses since 2006.
brand.year_founded_notes: Predecessor FEMO Group operated MosquitoNix businesses since 2006. Current franchisor entity formed June 26, 2023.
brand.year_first_franchised_notes: Began offering franchises December 2023. Predecessors offered MosquitoNix franchises off and on since 2008.

initial_franchise_fee.notes: Standard fee $49,000 for single territory (up to 60,000 targeted households at $100K+ avg income). Additional households at $0.81 each (up to 15,000 more). Non-refundable. VetFran discount: $2,500 off. Multi-territory discounts available: T2 $49K, T3-5 $44K each, T6-9 $39K each, T10+ $35K each. Refranchised business: no initial franchise fee. Additional fees at signing: Initial Training Fee $7,000, Opening Package $13,500-$14,500, Pre-Opening Marketing $2,000.

royalty_rate.notes: Tiered royalty: 10% on first $500K, 9% on $500K-$600K, 8% on $600K-$700K, 7% above $700K. Subject to minimum weekly royalty escalating from $0 (Year 1) to $387.50/week (Year 10). Greater of percentage or minimum applies. Paid weekly by EFT.

advertising_and_marketing.structure_description: Multi-component: Brand Development Fund 2% of Gross Sales + Customer Acquisition Commission 8% of single new sales + mandatory local marketing $42,000/year (Year 1), decreasing at higher revenue levels.

components.name: Brand Development Fund
components.notes: May increase to 3% on 60 days' notice.

components.name: Customer Acquisition Commission
components.basis: Gross Sales on single new sales
components.notes: 8% of Gross Sales for misting systems, QuickNix, warranty upgrades, pest control, holiday lighting single new sales.

components.name: Local Marketing Requirement
components.notes: Year 1: $42,000. Subsequent years: $42K if revenue <$500K, $21K if $500K-$700K, $16,800 if >$700K. Multiple contiguous territories: $15,000 per additional territory.

advertising_and_marketing.notes: Highest total marketing burden in the cohort when all components combined. The 8% Customer Acquisition Commission on new sales is unusual and substantial — effectively an additional royalty on new business revenue.

technology_fee.notes: $75/week (~$3,900/year). Cannot increase more than 20% per calendar year.

other_fees.notes: Currently none. If established, 1-2% of Gross Sales.

line_items.notes: MosquitoNix Pest & Holiday Lighting Systems, uniforms, yard signs.
5 days technical in Jacksonville FL + 3 days virtual business training. Up to 3 attendees.

tables.description: 2024 P&L for 4 company-affiliated markets operating with recommended management and marketing levels
tables.population: 4 company-affiliated (NOT franchised) markets: Jacksonville, Mid-Cities, North Dallas, Dallas. Each covers approximately 3 territories worth of households.
tables.notes: These are company-affiliated markets, NOT franchisees. None paid royalty or brand fund during the measurement period; figures show hypothetical franchise expense deductions. Sales per 60K-household territory ranged from $173,671 (North Dallas) to $578,939 (Dallas).

tables.description: 2024 P&L for 3 company-affiliated markets with less management/marketing spend
tables.population: 3 company-affiliated markets: West Coast FL, Orlando, Hilton Head.
tables.notes: Non-conforming markets spent less on management and marketing than recommended. 2 of 3 markets show negative net operating profit if franchised. Hilton Head is a sub-territory market (0.2 territories).

item_19_financial_performance.notes: Item 19 disclosed with detailed P&L data for 7 company-affiliated markets. CRITICAL CAVEAT: All data is from company-operated locations, not franchisees (0 franchisees had operated a full year as of the reporting period). Conforming markets show strong margins (25-35%); non-conforming markets show 2 of 3 losing money after hypothetical franchise fees. The 8% Customer Acquisition Commission is included in franchise expense deductions. Confidentiality clauses restrict certain former FEMO Group franchisees from discussing their experience.

years_reported.notes: Franchised: 0. All 7 outlets are company-owned (FL 3, SC 1, TX 3). No franchise system existed yet.
Franchised: 0. Began offering franchises in December 2023 but no units opened by year end.

Franchised: 0 start, 8 end. 10 franchised units opened but 2 ceased operations (South Carolina, 'Other Reasons'), leaving 8 at year end. Company-owned stable at 7. States: FL 1, GA 1, MO 1, NC 3, SC 1 (3 opened, 2 ceased), TN 1. Projected: 19 agreements signed not yet opened; 13 new franchised outlets projected for 2025.

source_fields: First year of franchising. 2 of 10 opened units ceased operations (20%), but denominator is debatable for a launch year.

derived.system_age_notes: Franchisor entity formed June 2023. Predecessor FEMO Group operated since 2006. Franchise system is functionally brand new.

warnings: Brand new franchise system: first franchised units opened in 2024. Only 8 franchised units at year end.
2 of 10 franchised units opened in 2024 ceased operations within the same year (South Carolina). 20% first-year attrition.
Royalty is tiered (10% down to 7%) — cannot be expressed as single rate.
8% Customer Acquisition Commission on new sales is unusual and substantial — effectively an additional royalty on new business.
Item 19 data is exclusively from company-operated locations, not franchisees. No franchisee had operated a full year.
Non-conforming markets show 2 of 3 locations unprofitable after hypothetical franchise fees.
$42,000 mandatory local marketing spend in Year 1 is the highest in the cohort.
Predecessor entity FEMO Group had franchisees subject to confidentiality clauses restricting discussion of their experience.
19 franchise agreements signed but not yet opened as of Dec 31, 2024 — aggressive growth pipeline.
Franchisor entity formed June 2023; predecessor operated since 2006. System age depends on which start date is used.

null_fields.reason: Tiered: 10% to 7% based on revenue bands. Cannot express as single rate.
Multi-component: 2% brand fund + 8% CAC + $42K local. No single rate.
Both royalty and marketing are multi-component.
First year of franchising. 2 cessations from 10 openings but no meaningful base for rate calculation.
